results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A FIELD-DEPLOYABLE, RAPID, SENSITIVE, AND AFFORDABLE SENSOR ON WHEELS (SOW) DEVICE, WHICH WILL BE CAPABLE OF DETECTING MULTIPLE BACTERIA TARGETS.ACTIVITIES:THE OBJECTIVES OF THE PROJECT ARE TO: 1) DESIGN AND SET UP THE SOW SYSTEM WITH A PORTABLE RAMAN SPECTROMETER AND MICROFLUIDIC CHIP, 2) VALIDATE THE PERFORMANCE WITH BACTERIA STANDARDS AND SPIKED ENVIRONMENTAL SAMPLES, 3) OPTIMIZE THE PERFORMANCE FOR LOCAL FIELD-TESTING, AND 4) FIELD-TEST THE SOW SYSTEM IN FLORIDA AND LOUISIANA BEFORE AND AFTER FLOODING/HURRICANES.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS. THE EXPECTED OUTCOME OF THIS PROJECT IS THE DEVELOPMENT AN AFFORDABLE, SENSITIVE, RAPID, AND FIELD-DEPLOYABLE SYSTEM FOR DETECTING VIABLE BACTERIAL PATHOGENS AND INDICATOR BACTERIA IN VARIOUS FIELD SETTINGS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE POPULATIONS IN FLOOD/HURRICANE-PRONE REGIONS, HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
